---
title: ""
page-layout: custom
---

::: {.home-hero}
::: {.hero-content}
# Urban Soil Biophysics

Research group at **Pontificia Universidad Católica de Chile** and [CEDEUS](https://cedeus.cl)

We study how urban soils store and transmit water and carbon, and how these processes can explain the biogeophysics of soils in the city.

[Our Research](projects.qmd){.btn-primary-usb} &nbsp; [Publications](publications.qmd){.btn-secondary-usb}
:::
:::

::: {.home-section}
## Focus Areas

::: {.focus-grid}
::: {.focus-card}
### Soil Water Retention
Characterization of soil water retention curves (SWRCs) in urban soils. 
:::

::: {.focus-card}
### Nature-Based Solutions
Hydrological performance of green infrastructure such as green roofs, urban parks, gardens, etc.
:::

::: {.focus-card}
### Urban Soil Health
Carbon dynamics, biological activity, and soil physical properties of urban soils. 
:::

:::
:::

::: {.home-section .section-muted}
## Institutional Context

We are part of [CEDEUS](https://cedeus.cl): Centro de Desarrollo Urbano Sustentable. A research centre hosted jointly by Pontificia Universidad Católica de Chile and Universidad de Concepción, funded by ANID. Our work links soil biophysics with urban planning, climate adaptation, and environmental policy. 

[About our people →](people.qmd)
:::
